hypotheses.title: Activation Volume Scaling Laws Predict ASD-Drug Mechanical Stability Under Stress
key_vulnerabilities: Competing mechanisms: particle fracture, stress-induced crystallization
Counter-evidence from manufacturing showing compression increases dissolution rate
Pressure effects may be overwhelmed by surface area changes
strengths: Sound theoretical TST extension to pressure
hypotheses.survival_reason: Interesting cross-disciplinary connection with sound theoretical basis and testable predictions

hypotheses.title: Silicate Network Modifier Analogies Predict Drug Loading Limits via Glass Transition Depression
key_vulnerabilities: Scale mismatch: Na+ ions vs drug molecules (1 Å vs 10-20 Å)
Different network types: ionic/covalent vs van der Waals/H-bonds
Mechanistic analogy may be superficial rather than fundamental
hypotheses.survival_reason: Survives due to novel framing and testability, but mechanistic analogy is questionable

hypotheses.title: Ionic Strength Buffering via Counterion Release Predicts pH-Independent ASD Dissolution
key_vulnerabilities: Low ionizable group density compared to mineral systems
Bulk solution pH effects may overwhelm microenvironment buffering
hypotheses.survival_reason: Interesting buffer theory application, but limited by inherently pH-dependent polymer behavior

hypotheses.title: Ostwald Ripening Competition Between LLPS and Crystallization Predicts Long-Term ASD Stability
key_vulnerabilities: Nucleation kinetics may dominate over growth kinetics
Drug absorption may remove material faster than ripening
hypotheses.survival_reason: Strong mechanistic foundation building logically on established theory with novel competitive insights

hypotheses.title: Congruent vs. Incongruent Dissolution Maps from Mineral Stoichiometry Predict ASD Release Mechanisms
key_vulnerabilities: Li & Taylor 2018 already observed congruent/incongruent ASD dissolution
May reduce to existing drug:polymer release ratio analysis with new vocabulary
Limited mechanistic advantage over current methods
strengths: Connects to established mineral dissolution concepts
hypotheses.survival_reason: Survives due to systematic framework, but novelty is primarily terminological

hypotheses.title: Reactive Surface Area Evolution from Fractal Scaling Laws Predicts ASD Dissolution Rate Deceleration
hypotheses.kill_reason: Fundamental mechanism mismatch. Fractal scaling laws developed for rigid mineral surfaces cannot describe swelling polymer gel formation. Forces inappropriate mathematical frameworks onto fundamentally different physics.
key_vulnerabilities: ASD dissolution involves polymer swelling and gel formation, not rigid surface roughening
Fractal scaling inappropriate for swelling systems
Two-stage model negates the fractal contribution

overall_assessment.cycle_comparison: 16.7% kill rate vs 42.9% in cycle 1 - improved quality but some forced analogies
overall_assessment.cross_pollination_success: All survivors successfully build on cycle 1 evolved insights
overall_assessment.strongest_hypothesis: H2.4 - Ostwald ripening competition with solid mechanistic foundation
main_vulnerabilities: Some terminological rather than conceptual novelty
overall_assessment.evolution_quality: Better theoretical grounding and experimental protocols vs raw cycle 1, but some analogies pushed beyond mechanistic validity

evolution_targets: H2.1: Resolve competing mechanisms (fracture vs pressure kinetics)
H2.2: Address scale mismatch between ionic and molecular network modifiers
H2.3: Define scope limitations due to pH-dependent polymer behavior
